question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with many spender.id=eq:X parameters where X values are spread across a wide range, causing the spender in ($5,...,$N) condition to prevent index usage on the spender column, forcing a sequential scan of all NFTs for the given account and exhausting database CPU?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes getQuery() to generate a UNION ALL query where the same spenderIdInFilters values are pushed into the shared params array once per subquery call (up to three times), causing the params array to contain duplicate parameter values at different indices, and the resulting SQL to reference many more bind parameters than necessary, increasing query parsing overhead?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNfts()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the coalesce(e.deleted, false) expression in nftQuery, which requires evaluating the entity table's deleted column for every row returned, by requesting a large number of NFTs across many distinct token IDs, causing the database to perform many entity table lookups and exhausting I/O resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the conditions array in getSubQuery() always starts with accountIdCondition (account_id = $1) and then appends all filter conditions, and by providing many filter conditions, cause the WHERE clause to become so large that PostgreSQL's query planner takes excessive time to optimize it, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the path where subQueries.length === 0 in getQuery() (all three filter arrays are empty) and simultaneously provide many spenderIdInFilters, causing getSubQuery() to be called once with an empty filters array but a large spenderIdInFilters, generating a query with a large IN clause that scans all NFTs for the account without any token_id or serial_number index restriction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNft()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests to getNft() with tokenId and serialNumber values that correspond to NFTs with maximum-size metadata fields, since nftByIdQuery uses select * which returns the full metadata BYTEA column, causing large memory allocations per request that accumulate under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NftService.columns maps filterKeys.SPENDER_ID to Nft.SPENDER ('spender'), and by providing both spenderIdFilters (range) and spenderIdInFilters (eq list), cause getSubQuery() to generate a WHERE clause with both a range condition and an IN condition on the same spender column, preventing the database from using a single index efficiently and causing a full table scan?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending many concurrent requests that each trigger the three-subquery UNION ALL path in getQuery(), since each request causes three separate database queries to be executed, multiplying the database load by a factor of three compared to single-subquery requests and exhausting the database connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNfts()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the absence of streaming in getNfts(), which loads the entire query result into memory via super.getRows() before processing, so that requesting the maximum limit of NFTs with large metadata fields causes a single large memory allocation that triggers Node.js out-of-memory errors?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the paramsForCondition array in getSubQuery() is built by mapping over spenderIdInFilters and pushing each value to params, and since params is shared across all getSubQuery() calls in getQuery(), the spenderIdInFilters values are pushed to params once per active subquery (up to three times), causing the params array to grow to 3x the number of spender eq filters and potentially exceeding PostgreSQL's parameter limit?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with token.id=gte:X&token.id=lte:Y&serialnumber=gt:A filters to activate all three subquery branches, combined with the maximum number of spender.id=eq:Z filters, causing getQuery() to generate a UNION ALL query where each subquery contains a large spender IN clause, and the total SQL string length exceeds tens of kilobytes, causing excessive memory allocation in both Node.js and PostgreSQL?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the order in which conditions are joined in getSubQuery() — where accountIdCondition is always first, followed by filter conditions, followed by the spender IN condition — to craft a query where the spender IN condition with many values prevents early row elimination by the account_id index, causing the database to scan more rows than necessary?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNfts()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests to getNfts() that generate UNION ALL queries, since each UNION ALL subquery independently applies limit $2, meaning the database may process up to 3*limit rows before the outer limit is applied, and by setting limit to its maximum value, cause the database to process three times the expected number of rows?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the nftQuery static string includes a left join with the entity table on every query, and by targeting an account whose NFTs reference token IDs that are not present in the entity table, cause the left join to perform many failed lookups that are not cached, exhausting database I/O?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the orderClause is constructed once in getQuery() and reused across all subqueries and the outer query, and if OrderSpec.from() does not properly validate the order parameter, cause an invalid order value to generate a malformed ORDER BY clause that causes a database error on every subquery execution?
